ORDER OF EXAMINATION ISSUED

Motion For Charging Order, Accounting, Assignment Order, And Temporary Restraining Order
Off Calendar Feb-15-2022 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFFS/judgment creditors CALVIN LOW and CALVIN IRA's Motion For Charging Order, Accounting, Assignment Order, And Temporary Restraining Order is granted and denied in part.

Plaintiffs' motion for a charging order is denied. California Code of Civil Procedure section 708.310 provides, "[i]f a money judgment is rendered against a partner or member but not against the partnership or limited liability company, the judgment debtor's interest in the partnership or limited liability company may be applied toward the satisfaction of the judgment by an order charging the judgment debtor's interest pursuant to Section 15907.03, 16504, or 17705.03 of the Corporations Code." (Emphasis added.)

A money judgment in favor of Plaintiffs was made jointly and severally against Defendants and judgment debtors Stella Capital Partners, L.P., Stella Capital, LLC, and Stella Advisors, LLC. While Plaintiffs establish that Stella Advisors is a general partner of Stella Capital Partners, the judgment was entered against a partner and the partnership (in addition to two LLCs). As such, Plaintiffs' requested relief under CCP section 708.310 and Corporations Code section 16504 is not available.

Plaintiffs' motion for an "accounting" pursuant to Corporations Code section 16504 is denied, as it is derivative of Plaintiffs' motion for a charging order.

Plaintiffs' motion for an assignment order is granted as unopposed. The payments assigned are as follows: all future payments from Stella Capital Partners, L.P. to Stella Advisors, not on account of Stella Advisors' partnership interest, but for services provided by Stella Advisors to Stella Capital Partners, L.P. as its general partner.

Plaintiffs' motion for a temporary restraining order is denied. The Court may issue such an order "upon a showing of need." (Cal. Code Civ. Proc. sec 708.520(b); see also In re Advanced Biomedical, Inc. (Bankr. C.D. Cal. 2016) 547 B.R. 337, 344 ["a section 708.520 restraining order is available only upon a showing a good cause."].) The threshold for making a showing a need is "relatively low." (Legal Additions LLC v. Kowalksi (N.D. Cal. 2011) WL 3156724, at *3 [granting restraining order under CCP 708.520 because defendants failed to make any payment on the judgment to plaintiff for over six months and because corporate defendant suspiciously dissolved soon after judgment was entered]; Linley Invs. v. Jamgotchian (C.D. Cal. 2019) WL 1429507, at *4 [granting restraining order based on evidence that defendant "engaged in certain conduct in the past that can reasonably be construed as an effort to evade the satisfaction of the Judgment."].)

Plaintiffs, however, do not meet even this low threshold. Plaintiffs only show that Defendants have failed to make any payments on the judgment for three months. Furthermore, Defendants do not oppose the assignment order. As such, Plaintiffs' request for an order restraining and prohibiting Stella Advisors and Stella Capital Partners from transferring to any third party or otherwise alienating any payments due under the assignment order is not warranted. The Court also denies Defendants' request for an undertaking as no restraining order has been granted.

Order is signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr., Clerk: S. Le, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/RBU)
